title: LED Blink Circuit
tips: Always use a current-limiting resistor with LEDs to prevent burning.
220Ω gives ~15mA at 5V — safe for standard LEDs.
Longer leg is anode (+), shorter leg is cathode (-).

title: Digital Dice (7-Segment Display)
connections: Display segments a-g → Arduino D2 to D8 via 220Ω resistors
Push Button Pin 2 → Arduino D9 (+ 10kΩ pull-down to GND)
tips: Make sure you configure segments correctly based on whether it is common cathode or common anode.
The randomSeed() ensures true pseudo-random sequences upon reset.

title: Traffic Light Controller
tips: Ensure clean timing loops. In industrial environments, hardware watchdog timers protect traffic controllers from locking up.
Use colored LEDs matching the lens to avoid spectrum attenuation.

title: RFID Smart Door Lock
connections: Green LED → D5, Red LED → D4
tips: Servo motor spikes can cause Arduino to reset; place a 470µF decoupling capacitor across the 5V and GND rail.
SPI lines must have short wire runs to prevent signal distortion.

title: LCD RFID Attendance Logger
connections: RFID SPI pins connected to D10-D13
tips: DS3231 is highly accurate because of internal temperature compensation.
The I2C bus supports multiple devices by allocating unique slave addresses (LCD = 0x27, RTC = 0x68).

title: Matrix Keypad Combination Lock
tips: Keypads use matrix scanning to save GPIO pins — 8 pins read 16 buttons.
Add a mechanical debouncer in code to prevent double keypress triggers.

title: PIR Intruder Alarm System
tips: PIR sensors require a warm-up period of 30-60 seconds to calibrate to the background infrared signature.
Adjust the sensitivity potentiometer on the sensor body to scale detection ranges.

title: Stopwatch with LCD and Interrupts
connections: LCD pins connected via standard 6-wire parallel bus (D4-D7, RS, EN)
tips: Use 'volatile' keyword for variables modified inside ISRs to prevent the compiler from caching them.
Hardware debouncers (RC filter) are critical for interrupt buttons to avoid false triggers.

title: LM35 Precision Temperature Monitor
tips: LM35 linear scale: 10mV/°C. A 0.5V output corresponds to 50°C.
Configure Arduino analog reference to INTERNAL (1.1V) to improve resolution for low temperatures.

title: Temperature-Controlled DC Fan
connections:: 1N4007 Diode Cathode → DC Fan (+), Anode → Fan (-)
tips: A TIP120 Darlington transistor is ideal for amplification because of its high current gain (hFE).
The flyback diode suppresses back-EMF spikes when turning off the DC fan.
